title: Green Thumb Industries Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2022 Results
text: Green Thumb Industries reached a significant milestone in 2022, achieving $1 billion in annual revenue—a 13.9% increase from the prior year. The company demonstrated strong operational performance despite market headwinds, reporting adjusted operating EBITDA of $311 million, representing 31% of revenue. For Q4 2022, revenue totaled $259.3 million, up 6.4% year-over-year but down 1% sequentially. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $51.2 million, primarily due to an $88.5 million non-cash impairment charge related to its Nevada business. Excluding this impairment, adjusted net income reached $12 million with adjusted operating EBITDA of $81.2 million. The company maintained strong liquidity with $177.7 million in cash at year-end and generated $159 million in operating cash flow for the full year. Comparable store sales grew 3.4% in Q4, while overall retail revenue increased 24.1% annually. CEO Ben Kovler noted that units sold continues to climb, up 28% year-over-year. Gross margins compressed in 2022, declining to 49.5% of revenue from 55.1% in 2021, reflecting industry-wide pricing pressures.
